name: db-writer
description: Writes processed records to relational databases. Supports INSERT, UPSERT, and MERGE strategies across PostgreSQL, SQL Server, and MySQL.
author: hermes-core

connection_string.title: Connection String
connection_string.description: Full ADO.NET connection string for the target database

provider.title: Database Provider
provider.description: Relational database provider to write to

pool_min_size.title: Connection Pool Minimum Size
pool_min_size.description: Minimum number of idle connections maintained in the pool

pool_max_size.title: Connection Pool Maximum Size
pool_max_size.description: Maximum number of concurrent connections allowed in the pool

command_timeout_seconds.title: Command Timeout (seconds)
command_timeout_seconds.description: Maximum time in seconds to wait for a write command to complete

retry_max_attempts.title: Retry Max Attempts
retry_max_attempts.description: Maximum number of retry attempts on transient database errors

retry_delay_seconds.title: Retry Delay (seconds)
retry_delay_seconds.description: Initial delay in seconds between retry attempts (exponential backoff applied)

schema_name.title: Schema Name
schema_name.description: Database schema that contains the destination table

write_mode.title: Write Mode
write_mode.description: Strategy used when writing records to the table

conflict_key.title: Conflict Key Column(s)
conflict_key.description: Comma-separated column name(s) used to detect duplicates in UPSERT or MERGE mode

column_mapping.title: Column Mapping
column_mapping.description: Map input record fields to destination column names (key: source field, value: target column). Omit to use field names as-is.

batch_size.title: Batch Size
batch_size.description: Number of records written per database transaction

create_table_if_missing.title: Create Table If Missing
create_table_if_missing.description: Automatically create the destination table based on the inferred record schema if it does not exist

truncate_before_write.title: Truncate Before Write
truncate_before_write.description: Truncate the destination table before writing the first batch (useful for full-refresh loads)
